label: Contrastive learning

node.label: Contrastive learning
node.definition: Representations are shaped by pulling related examples together and separating unrelated examples.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Contrastive learning is to connect Self-supervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations to downstream capabilities including Contrastive and mutual-information objectives.
examples: substrate for Contrastive and mutual-information objectives
limitations: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Self-supervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations.

dependencies.rationale: Contrastive learning follows the learning setup expressed by Self-supervised learning.
Contrastive learning uses Embeddings & distributed representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Contrastive learning uses Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Contrastive learning can also be used in supervised or weakly supervised settings when labels define the positive and negative pairs.
